title: Slitting arbor bearing replacement
description: High vibration alarm triggered at 5.2 mm/s on slitting head. Audible grinding noise from arbor. Bearing FAG 6210-2RS failed due to missed lubrication schedule (3 weeks without grease).
root_cause: Lubrication missed for 3 consecutive weeks. Grease schedule not followed by C-shift operator.
actions_taken: 1. Machine stopped and LOTO applied. 2. Blade assembly removed. 3. Old bearings extracted with puller. 4. Arbor shaft cleaned and inspected. 5. New FAG 6210-2RS bearings pressed in (4 nos). 6. Blade assembly reinstalled with correct spacers. 7. Test run successful. Vibration: 1.2 mm/s.
preventive_recommendations: Enforce daily grease schedule. Add reminder in shift handover checklist.

title: Slitting blade #3 chipped — replacement
description: Burr formation on slit strip edge #3. Blade inspection revealed chip on cutting edge approximately 3mm × 1mm.
root_cause: Hard inclusion (oxide) in steel coil from supplier Batch #CR-2024-0892. Supplier notification sent.
actions_taken: 1. LOTO applied. 2. Blade assembly removed. 3. Blade #3 replaced with new. 4. Spacer stack verified. 5. Torque applied 180 Nm. 6. Test strip quality: OK.
preventive_recommendations: Enhance incoming coil inspection. Reject coils with visible inclusions.

title: IGBT module failure — HF welder inverter
description: Inverter tripped during production with fault code F-201 (IGBT Short Circuit). Welding stopped completely. Power surge during thunderstorm suspected.
root_cause: Lightning-induced power transient on 11kV supply. Existing surge protection inadequate for the event magnitude.
actions_taken: 1. Inverter isolated and DC bus discharged (waited 15 min). 2. IGBT module #3 identified as failed (visual burn mark). 3. Gate driver board also damaged. 4. Both replaced with spares. 5. Inverter parameters re-loaded from backup. 6. Test weld: OK. 7. Surge protector upgrade ordered.
preventive_recommendations: Install Type-2 surge protection on 11kV incoming. Keep 2 IGBT modules in stock.

title: Induction coil #3 ground fault — heat treatment furnace
description: Furnace tripped on ground fault detection during heating cycle. Coil #3 insulation breakdown detected. Water traces found at coil terminal joint.
root_cause: Water ingress at coil terminal brazing joint. Joint deterioration due to thermal cycling (6 years of operation). Insulation resistance dropped to 0.2 MΩ (minimum acceptable: 10 MΩ).
actions_taken: 1. Furnace isolated at 11kV breaker. 2. Coil #3 disconnected. 3. Old coil removed (crane assist). 4. New coil installed and connected. 5. Busbar re-torqued. 6. Megger test: 45 MΩ (PASS). 7. Water connections checked — no leaks. 8. Furnace restarted, temperature profile verified. 9. Joint sealing procedure improved with high-temp silicone.
preventive_recommendations: Monthly megger test on all coils. Inspect joints with thermal camera weekly. Replace coils preventively at 15,000 operating hours.

title: Main motor bearing replacement — forming mill
description: Vibration reading on main motor drive end (DE) bearing reached 6.2 mm/s RMS. Spectrum analysis shows BPFO frequency dominant — outer race fault developing.
root_cause: Bearing outer race spalling detected. Bearing had been in service for 6.5 years (approximately 35,000 hours). Normal fatigue life exceeded.
actions_taken: 1. Motor isolated and LOTO. 2. Motor uncoupled from gearbox. 3. Motor moved to workshop. 4. Both DE and NDE bearings replaced. 5. Motor reinstalled and aligned (laser alignment: 0.02mm). 6. Test run: vibration 1.8 mm/s RMS (excellent).
preventive_recommendations: Schedule motor bearing replacement every 5 years (preventive). Add vibration trending to monthly checklist.

title: Hydraulic pump rebuild
description: Hydraulic pressure dropping to 85 bar during decoiler operation (normal: 120-160 bar). Decoiler mandrel grip slipping. Pump making unusual noise.
root_cause: Pump swashplate worn. Oil analysis showed high particle count (ISO 22/20/17 — limit is 18/16/13). Oil change overdue by 2 months.

parts_used.description: Pump rebuild kit (swashplate, pistons, valve plate)

actions_taken: 1. System drained. 2. Pump removed from HPU. 3. Pump disassembled — swashplate showing 0.3mm wear (limit: 0.1mm). 4. Rebuild kit installed. 5. Tank cleaned. 6. New filters installed. 7. System filled with fresh Mobil DTE 25 (380 L). 8. Air bled. 9. Pressure test: 160 bar — OK. 10. All hydraulic functions verified.
preventive_recommendations: Strictly follow quarterly oil analysis. Change oil per schedule. Install online particle counter for continuous monitoring.

title: Squeeze roller bearing replacement
description: Vibration on squeeze stand reached 5.8 mm/s. Audible rumbling from lower squeeze roller. Weld quality showing minor misalignment.
root_cause: Bearing degradation due to scale contamination. Bearing seal damaged, allowing mill scale particles to enter.
actions_taken: 1. Welding line stopped and LOTO. 2. Squeeze stand disassembled. 3. Lower roller bearings replaced. 4. New seals installed. 5. Roller reinstalled and aligned. 6. Test weld: vibration 1.5 mm/s, weld quality OK.
preventive_recommendations: Install additional scale guards around squeeze stand. Monthly seal inspection.

title: Inverter IGBT failure — furnace coil #5
description: Furnace tripped with fault code ERR-302 on coil #5 inverter. IGBT module short-circuit detected. Power surge from main transformer.
root_cause: Power surge during grid voltage fluctuation. Line reactor not installed on coil #5 inverter (installed on other 5 coils after previous incident).
actions_taken: 1. Inverter isolated. 2. DC bus discharged. 3. Failed IGBT replaced. 4. Line reactor installed on coil #5 input. 5. All parameters re-loaded. 6. Test run: coil #5 heating uniformly. 7. Now all 6 coils have line reactors.
preventive_recommendations: Completed — all 6 inverters now have line reactors. Annual IGBT inspection added to PM schedule.

title: Hydrostatic test pump motor bearing replacement
description: Vibration on hydro test pump motor reached 5.8 mm/s. Pump pressure fluctuating. Motor running hot (82°C).
root_cause: Motor DE bearing degraded (operating life 35,000 hrs). Water mist from hydro test area causing mild corrosion on bearing housing.
actions_taken: 1. Pump isolated and LOTO. 2. Motor uncoupled. 3. Both bearings replaced. 4. Motor reinstalled and aligned. 5. Splash guard installed on motor. 6. Test: vibration 2.1 mm/s, pressure stable.
preventive_recommendations: Install motor splash guard permanently. Schedule bearing replacement every 3 years.

title: All universal joint spider replacement (preventive)
description: Accelerated wear on UJ spiders #2, #6, #7 detected during inspection. Decision to replace all 14 spiders preventively during planned shutdown.
root_cause: Heavy pipe production (75mm heavy wall) causing higher torque load on universal joints. Wear accelerated from normal 6-month life to 3 months.
actions_taken: 1. Mill stopped during planned Saturday PM. 2. All 14 UJ shafts disconnected. 3. All spider bearings replaced. 4. Shafts reconnected and pin clips installed. 5. Test run at 20 m/min: smooth operation, no vibration.
preventive_recommendations: Reduce PM interval for UJ spiders to quarterly during heavy pipe production campaigns.

title: Scheduled arbor bearing replacement (preventive PM)
description: Vibration trending up from baseline 1.5 to 3.8 mm/s over 3 months. Bearings at 4800 operating hours. Scheduled replacement per new PM policy.
root_cause: Normal wear — bearings approaching end of reliable service life (5000 hr policy).
actions_taken: 1. Planned shutdown. 2. Bearings replaced per standard procedure. 3. Post-replacement vibration: 1.1 mm/s. 4. Next replacement scheduled at 10,000 cumulative hours.
preventive_recommendations: Current 5000-hr replacement policy is effective. Continue.

title: Straightener roll re-chroming
description: Pipe surface showing roller marks after straightening. Roll #3 and #4 worn beyond limit (surface roughness Ra > 3.2µm, limit: 1.6µm).
root_cause: Normal wear after 2 years of continuous heavy-duty straightening. Chrome plating worn through on roll #3 and #4.
actions_taken: 1. Straightener stopped. 2. Rolls #3, #4 removed. 3. Spare rolls #3, #4 installed. 4. Worn rolls sent for re-chroming (will be returned in 10 days as spares). 5. New rolls alignment verified. 6. Pipe quality check: no marks.
preventive_recommendations: Keep one set of spare straightener rolls always ready. Check roll surface monthly.
